mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of falling FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant excess return versus periods of rising FEDFUNDS (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration consistent with recession dynamics, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7 with prior sharp rise through the 2008-2009 recession
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects simultaneous weakness in both durable and non-durable consumer demand channels, a combined signal stronger than either series alone, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as aggregate demand contraction feeds into earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change < 0 and RSAFS 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1372K near historic lows in mid-2009
failure_condition: The joint-decline signal underperforms a model using HOUST or RSAFS individually in out-of-sample Rank IC, or triggered-period returns are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During an early post-recession recovery regime, stabilization or upturn in INDPRO after a prolonged decline signals the trough of the manufacturing cycle, leading investors to price in a production-led recovery and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.6651 following sharp 2008-2009 industrial contraction
regime_hint: early recovery from recession / cyclical trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO stabilization are not significantly different from unconditional returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS), even amid an overall declining trend, provides a leading read on near-term labor demand that is quickly incorporated into equity prices, such that a deceleration in the monthly rate of payroll decline predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157087K with decelerating month-over-month job losses in mid-2009
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month deceleration is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) across at least two independent rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below a critical accommodative threshold and remains there, the market anticipates a prolonged period of cheap financing that disproportionately benefits equity valuations, generating a sustained positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following month that is stronger than the effect of gradual rate declines alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and stays below for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Conditional 20-day mean return during sub-1% FEDFUNDS periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional 20-day mean return (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one historical sub-period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL alongside stagnant or declining RSAFS signals emerging stagflation pressure where nominal price increases are not matched by real consumption growth, eroding real household purchasing power and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as margin compression risk rises.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 2% while RSAFS 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 20-day forward returns not significantly more negative than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the pattern fails to replicate in at least two distinct historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a rising UNRATE combined with a stable or falling FEDFUNDS reflects continued labor market slack met by supportive monetary policy, a policy-put dynamic that supports risk assets and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter despite weak labor data, because markets price forward policy support over current labor conditions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE trending up while FEDFUNDS is flat or declining for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7 amid ongoing recession recovery expectations
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with accommodative monetary backstop
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE and flat/falling FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the joint-condition signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC.
